title: Enzo Cannavale

infobox.value: Vincenzo Cannavale  (1928-04-05)5 April 1928  [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia"), [Campania](/wiki/Campania "Campania"), Italy

* [Gino Cervi](/wiki/Gino_Cervi "Gino Cervi") (1946) * [Massimo Serato](/wiki/Massimo_Serato "Massimo Serato") (1947) * [Nando Bruno](/wiki/Nando_Bruno "Nando Bruno") (1948) * [Saro Urzì](/wiki/Saro_Urz%C3%AC "Saro Urzì") (1949) * [Umberto Spadaro](/wiki/Umberto_Spadaro "Umberto Spadaro") (1951) * [Gabriele Ferzetti](/wiki/Gabriele_Ferzetti "Gabriele Ferzetti") (1953) * [Alberto Sordi](/wiki/Alberto_Sordi "Alberto Sordi") (1954) * [Paolo Stoppa](/wiki/Paolo_Stoppa "Paolo Stoppa") (1955) * [Memmo Carotenuto](/wiki/Memmo_Carotenuto "Memmo Carotenuto") (1956) * [Peppino De Filippo](/wiki/Peppino_De_Filippo "Peppino De Filippo") (1957) * [Andrea Checchi](/wiki/Andrea_Checchi "And

"**Enzo**" **Cannavale** (5 April 1928 – 18 March 2011)[[1]](#cite_note-tgcom-1) was an Italian film actor. He appeared in more than 100 films since 1949, including *[Cinema Paradiso](/wiki/Cinema_Paradiso "Cinema Paradiso")*, which won the [Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Foreign_Language_Film "Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film") at the [62nd Academy Awards](/wiki/62nd_Academy_Awards "62nd Academy Awards") in 1990.[[2]](#cite_note-Oscars1990-2) He was awarded the [Nastro d’Argento for Best Supporting Actor](/wiki/Nastro_d%27Argento_Best_supporting_Actor "Nastro d'Argento Best supporting Actor") in *[32 dicembre](/wiki/32_dicembre "32 dicembre")* (*December 32nd*) by [Luciano De Crescenzo](/wiki/Luciano_De_Crescenzo "Luciano De Crescenzo").

["E' morto Enzo Cannavale"](https://web.archive.org/web/20110828000746/http://www.tgcom.mediaset.it/spettacolo/articoli/1003529/cinema-morto-enzo-cannavale.shtml). *tgcom*. Archived from [the original](http://www.tgcom.mediaset.it/spettacolo/articoli/1003529/cinema-morto-enzo-cannavale.shtml) on 28 August 2011. Retrieved 17 March 2011.
["The 62nd Academy Awards (1990) Nominees and Winners"](http://www.oscars.org/oscars/ceremonies/1990). *oscars.org*. Retrieved 17 October 2011.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actor (1928–2011)

| Enzo Cannavale | |
| --- | --- |
| Cannavale in *[La liceale](/wiki/La_liceale "La liceale")* (1975) | |
| Born | Vincenzo Cannavale  (1928-04-05)5 April 1928  [Castellammare di Stabia](/wiki/Castellammare_di_Stabia "Castellammare di Stabia"), [Campania](/wiki/Campania "Campania"), Italy |
| Died | 18 March 2011(2011-03-18) (aged 82)  [Naples](/wiki/Naples "Naples"), Campania, Italy |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1949–2011 |
| Height | 1.58 m (5 ft 2 in) |
| Relatives | [Bobby Cannavale](/wiki/Bobby_Cannavale "Bobby Cannavale") (nephew) [Jake Cannavale](/wiki/Jake_Cannavale "Jake Cannavale") (grandnephew) |

**Vincenzo** "**Enzo**" **Cannavale** (5 April 1928 – 18 March 2011)[[1]](#cite_note-tgcom-1) was an Italian film actor. He appeared in more than 100 films since 1949, including *[Cinema Paradiso](/wiki/Cinema_Paradiso "Cinema Paradiso")*, which won the [Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film](/wiki/Academy_Award_for_Best_Foreign_Language_Film "Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film") at the [62nd Academy Awards](/wiki/62nd_Academy_Awards "62nd Academy Awards") in 1990.[[2]](#cite_note-Oscars1990-2) He was awarded the [Nastro d’Argento for Best Supporting Actor](/wiki/Nastro_d%27Argento_Best_supporting_Actor "Nastro d'Argento Best supporting Actor") in *[32 dicembre](/wiki/32_dicembre "32 dicembre")* (*December 32nd*) by [Luciano De Crescenzo](/wiki/Luciano_De_Crescenzo "Luciano De Crescenzo").

## Selected filmography

* *Sogno di una notte di mezza sbornia* (1959) - Fish-monger
* *[Leoni al sole](/wiki/Leoni_al_sole "Leoni al sole")* (1961) - Il commissario
* *[The Four Days of Naples](/wiki/The_Four_Days_of_Naples_(film) "The Four Days of Naples (film)")* (1962) - Partigiano (uncredited)
* *[Treasure of San Gennaro](/wiki/Treasure_of_San_Gennaro "Treasure of San Gennaro")* (1966) - Gaetano
